title: Make encrypted field rotation survive authenticated failures
prompt: The encrypted-field migration already stores a key version in each AES-GCM envelope, can decrypt both configured legacy and current versions, skips current-version rows on subsequent runs, and uses compare-and-swap persistence. However, one corrupt legacy envelope aborts the entire batch. Update the rotation implementation so an AES-GCM authentication failure is isolated to that record: leave its persisted envelope untouched, record its ID in FailedRecordIds, emit a warning that identifies the record ID and stored key version using only operational metadata (never plaintext), and continue rotating later records. A later run must still skip records already rotated and migrate a recovered legacy record. Do not swallow configuration errors such as a missing key version, keep the public contracts intact, and do not modify the protected tests. Verification is offline and succeeds with the declared verify command from the repository root.
verify_cmd: env DOTNET_CLI_HOME=/tmp/moonshiner-dotnet-home NUGET_PACKAGES=/tmp/moonshiner-nuget-packages DOTNET_SKIP_FIRST_TIME_EXPERIENCE=1 DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT=1 DOTNET_NOLOGO=1 dotnet run --project tests/EncryptedFieldRotation.ProtectedTests/EncryptedFieldRotation.ProtectedTests.csproj --configuration Release --artifacts-path /tmp/moonshiner-dotnet-artifacts --disable-build-servers
